nameMeanings.name: BASSETT
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from Old French basset ‘of low stature’, a diminutive of basse ‘low, short’, either a nickname for a short person or a status name for someone of humble origins.
Altered form of French Bessette 1 or Besset (see Bessette 2).

History: William Bassett (c. 1598–1667) came to Plymouth, MA, from Kent, England, in the 1620s; c. 1650 he moved to Duxbury and subesequently to Bridgewater. He had many prominent descendants, among them one of the earliest families on Martha's Vineyard. — The surname Bassett of French origin (see 2 above) is listed in the register of Huguenot ancestors recognized by the Huguenot Society of America.

variants.name: Bessette
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Marcel, Armand, Adrien, Normand, Alphonse, Lucien, Cecile, Herve, Jacques, Laurent, Aime.

French: topographic name designating a small wood of birches, from Occitan bès ‘birch tree’ (see Besse ), or a habitational name from (La) Bessette, the name of several places in the southern part of France.
Alternatively, an altered form of French Besset: topographic name, a cognate of 1 above. Altered ending reflects the Canadian and American French practice of sounding the final -t.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT was born in 1280, in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England. She had at least 1 son with Thomas Stafford. She died in 1337, in her hometown, at the age of 57.

fr.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT est née en 1280, à Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Angleterre. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils avec Thomas Stafford. Elle est décédée en 1337, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 57 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT wurde 1280 in Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England geboren. Sie hatte mit Thomas Stafford mindestens 1 Sohn. Sie starb 1337 im Alter von 57 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT è nata il nel 1280 nel Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, England. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio con Thomas Stafford. Morì nel 1337, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 57 anni.

ko.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT(은)는 1280년 영국 스태퍼드실르 드라이턴 배싯에서 태어났다. 그녀는 Thomas Stafford(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그녀는 1337년 그녀의 고향에서 57세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT nasceu em 1280, em Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Ela teve pelo menos 1 filho com Thomas Stafford. Ela faleceu em 1337, em sua cidade natal, com 57 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT родилась в 1280 г. в местности дреитон бассетт, стаффордшир, Англия. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын от Thomas Stafford. Она умерла в 1337 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 57 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Lady Margaret BASSETT nació en 1280, en Drayton Bassett, Staffordshire, Inglaterra. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Thomas Stafford. Murió en 1337, en su pueblo, a la edad de 57 años.
